name: Madeleine Loop
name_original: Boucle entre Camou et La Madelaine
description: This difficult 19.7-mile mountain biking loop, Boucle entre Camou et La Madelaine, offers challenging terrain and scenic views.
region_intro: Hiking around Menditte offers access to the foothills of the Pyrenees, characterized by rolling hills, forested areas, and elevated points providing extensive views. The region features a network of trails that traverse varied terrain, including paths leading to summits and historical chapels. This landscape provides opportunities for both moderate ascents and scenic loops through natural surroundings. Best hiking trails around Menditte The most popular hiking route is Erretzü summit loop from Chapelle de la Madeleine, a 5.0 miles (8.0 km) trail that takes 2 hours 56 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers summit views and passes by a notable chapel. Another top favourite among local hikers is Tardets – Panorama 360° loop from Alos-Sibas-Abense, a moderate 7.3 miles (11.8 km) path. This trail provides expansive panoramic views of the surrounding landscape. Local hikers also love the Great View of the Pyrenees loop from Ordiarp, a 6.3 miles (10.2 km) trail leading through scenic terrain with clear views of the Pyrenees, often completed in about 3 hours 3 minutes. Hiking around Menditte is defined by panoramic views, summit ascents, and routes through the Pyrenean foothills. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in Menditte are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 80 reviews. More than 400 hikers have used komoot to explore Menditte's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Menditte?
A: Menditte offers a selection of over 50 hiking routes. These trails traverse the foothills of the Pyrenees, providing varied terrain from rolling hills to forested areas and elevated viewpoints.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners in Menditte?
A: Yes, Menditte has 16 easy hiking trails. These routes are ideal for beginners or those looking for a more relaxed walk through the scenic Pyrenean foothills.

Q: What kind of views can I expect on hikes around Menditte?
A: Hiking around Menditte is characterized by panoramic views, especially of the Pyrenees. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10525175" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Great View of the Pyrenees loop from Ordiarp&lt;/a&gt; offer clear vistas of the mountain range, while others provide extensive views from elevated points.

Q: Are there any circular walks in Menditte?
A: Many of the trails in Menditte are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/993216" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Erretzü summit loop from Chapelle de la Madeleine&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/3992907" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tardets – Panorama 360° loop from Alos-Sibas-Abense&lt;/a&gt;, both offering diverse scenery.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Menditte?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 80 reviews. Hikers often praise the stunning panoramic views, the varied terrain, and the opportunity to explore the Pyrenean foothills.

Q: Are there any notable landmarks or attractions to see along the hiking trails?
A: Yes, several trails pass by interesting landmarks. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/993216" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Erretzü summit loop from Chapelle de la Madeleine&lt;/a&gt; passes by a notable chapel. You can also find highlights like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/797632" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Panorama 360°&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3368769" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Madeleine Chapel&lt;/a&gt; in the vicinity.

Q: What is the typical duration for a moderate hike in Menditte?
A: Moderate hikes in Menditte typically range from 2 to 4 hours. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10525175" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Great View of the Pyrenees loop from Ordiarp&lt;/a&gt; is about 3 hours 3 minutes, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/41477893" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Erretzü summit – At the summit of Erretzu loop from Alos-Sibas-Abense&lt;/a&gt; takes around 3 hours 47 minutes.

Q: Are there any trails that offer summit ascents?
A: Yes, Menditte offers routes that include summit ascents. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/993216" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Erretzü summit loop from Chapelle de la Madeleine&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate trail that provides summit views.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Menditte?
A: The foothills of the Pyrenees, including Menditte, are generally pleasant for hiking from spring through autumn. These seasons offer milder temperatures and vibrant natural scenery. Winter hiking is possible but may require appropriate gear due to potential snow or colder conditions at higher elevations.

Q: Are there any trails suitable for families with children?
A: While specific 'family-friendly' designations are not listed, the 16 easy trails available in Menditte are generally suitable for families looking for less strenuous walks. These routes often feature gentle inclines and scenic surroundings.

Q: Can I find trails that pass through forested areas?
A: Yes, Menditte's landscape includes forested areas. Many trails weave through these woods, offering shade and a different kind of natural beauty compared to the open panoramic viewpoints.

Q: What is the longest moderate trail available in Menditte?
A: Among the moderate trails, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/3992907" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tardets – Panorama 360° loop from Alos-Sibas-Abense&lt;/a&gt; is one of the longer options, covering 11.8 km (7.3 miles) and typically taking around 3 hours 58 minutes to complete.
tips: Borrowed this summer on gravel, goes very well with 40 tires, also a paradise for mountain bikers. Splendid landscapes and quite a few animals in the nearby pastures!

Very nice climb from Tardets with some rather severe steep slopes. About 7km of climb on a fairly narrow road at 8/10% with passages at 16 or 18%. The coating is not bad at all even if the end is covered with sheep droppings :)
The landscape is absolutely magnificent, a 360 degree panorama over Béarn, the Basque country, the peaks of Anie, the Midi d'Ossau, the plain of Pau and even the ocean on a clear day.

faqs.question: How challenging is the Boucle entre Camou et La Madelaine mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as difficult. It covers a distance of approximately 31.6 km with over 1,000 meters of elevation gain, requiring very good fitness and advanced riding skills. You might even need to push your bike in some sections due to the challenging terrain.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: On average, completing this difficult mountain bike loop takes about 3 hours and 13 minutes. However, your actual time may vary depending on your fitness level, riding pace, and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain on this route is varied and can be quite rugged. Expect unpaved sections, dirt forestry roads, and rough gravel roads, especially at higher altitudes. Some parts might even be affected by meltwater or become boggy, indicating potentially challenging and less maintained conditions.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest or viewpoints along the route?
faqs.answer: Along the route, you'll encounter stunning alpine scenery, including forests and meadows, with picturesque views of the surrounding peaks. Key highlights include the Gravel to the foot of the Oxibar pass and the historic Madeleine Chapel. You'll also pass through authentic hamlets that add to the charm of the ride.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Boucle entre Camou et La Madelaine?
faqs.answer: While specific parking details for the Boucle entre Camou et La Madelaine are not provided, it's generally advisable to look for designated parking areas in or near Camou, the starting point of the loop. Local village centers often have public parking available.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this loop?
faqs.answer: The best time to mountain bike this route is typically during the warmer months, from late spring to early autumn. This avoids snow and ice at higher elevations and ensures more stable trail conditions. Always check local weather forecasts before heading out, as conditions can change rapidly in mountainous areas.

faqs.question: Is the Boucle entre Camou et La Madelaine dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While many trails in the region are dog-friendly, specific regulations for this particular route are not detailed. It's always best to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas with livestock or wildlife, and to pack out all waste. Check local signage for any specific restrictions.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits or pay an entrance fee for mountain biking in Soule?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for mountain biking on public trails in the Soule region. However, always respect private property, follow local regulations, and be mindful of other trail users. If you plan to enter any specific natural reserves or parks, check their individual rules.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several other official trails. You'll find yourself on parts of the Boucle de la Madeleine, Boucle de Camou, and Le tour de Soule, among others. It also briefly touches upon La grotte des Laminak and Bizkaiko bidea.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or accommodation near the trail?
faqs.answer: While the route itself is a mountain biking loop, the villages of Camou and La Madelaine, and the broader Soule region, offer various facilities. You can find cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options in nearby hamlets and towns, perfect for before or after your ride.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for electric mountain bikes (e-MTBs)?
faqs.answer: Given its 'difficult' classification and significant elevation gain, this route could be a good challenge for e-MTB riders looking for a strenuous workout. The assistance from an e-MTB could make the demanding climbs more manageable, but advanced riding skills will still be essential for navigating the rugged terrain.

waypoints.name: Gravel to the foot of the Oxibar pass
